service.name: Google Maps Platform
service.description_for_ai: World's most widely used mapping and location API. Covers geocoding, places search, directions, distance matrix, and static maps. Global coverage. Used by millions of apps. Best for international use cases outside Russia.

auth.how_to_get: Create project at https://console.cloud.google.com/ and enable Maps APIs
auth.cost: Free: $200/month credit. Geocoding: $5/1000 requests. Places: $17/1000 requests

capabilities.description_for_ai: Convert address to coordinates (forward) or coordinates to address (reverse). Best geocoder globally. Use for any address-to-coordinates task outside Russia (for Russia prefer Yandex Maps).

address.description: Address for forward geocoding. E.g. '1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA'

capabilities.description_for_ai: Find places (restaurants, shops, hospitals, etc.) near coordinates. Returns name, rating, opening hours, price level. Use for 'restaurants nearby', 'find a pharmacy', 'coffee shops around here'.

type.description: Place type: restaurant, cafe, pharmacy, hospital, bank, supermarket, gas_station etc.

language.description: Language code: en, ru, de, fr, es, zh, ja, ko, pt

capabilities.description_for_ai: Get directions between two points. Returns route steps, duration, distance, and travel mode options. Use for 'how to get from A to B', 'driving directions', 'transit route'.

language.description: Language code: en, ru, de, fr, es, zh, ja, ko, pt

agent_hints.vs_yandex: For Russia use Yandex Maps (better Russian address data). Google Maps is better for international locations.
agent_hints.place_types: Common types: restaurant, cafe, pharmacy, hospital, bank, supermarket, gas_station, gym, hotel, school, airport, subway_station.
agent_hints.coords_format: Google uses 'lat,lng' format (latitude first) — opposite of 2GIS and Yandex which use 'lng,lat'.
agent_hints.pricing_tip: Each API has separate pricing. Geocoding and Directions are cheapest. Places Nearby is more expensive — use sparingly.

limits.restriction: Must display results on Google Maps — cannot use data without the map
